Acr.description:
Admin command report.

- Contains the raw command text and result.

admin.description: Whether the user is an admin.

AiInput.description: AI input control value with optional duration.

oneOf.description: Repeat AI Information at a given interval. 0 to stop.

Aic.description:
AI input control packet.

- Sends a sequence of AI input commands.

inputs.description: Inputs to send (use [AiInput] helpers to build common inputs).

Aii.description:
AI telemetry snapshot.

- Returned in response to AI info requests.

plid.description: Player id of the AI car.

AngVel.description:
Angular velocity stored as radians per second (f32).

- `from_wire_i16` / `to_wire_i16` use the LFS scaling (16384 = 360°/s).
- Positive values indicate clockwise rotation when viewed from above.
- Stored as `f32` radians/sec for consistency with [`Heading`](crate::heading::Heading)
  and [`Speed`](crate::speed::Speed).

Axi.description:
AutoX layout summary.

- Reports counts for objects and checkpoints plus the last loaded layout name.
- Can be requested via [`TinyType::Axi`](crate::insim::TinyType::Axi).

lname.description: Name of the last loaded layout (if loaded locally).

Axm.description:
AutoX multiple objects update.

Adds, removes, or reports layout objects. Action-specific flags are on
[`flags`](Axm::flags); the action and its data are on [`action`](Axm::action).

action.description: The action and its associated objects or data.

ucid.description: Connection that sent or requested the update.

Axo.description:
AutoX object contact event.

- Sent when an autocross object is hit.

Bfn.description:
Button management command or notification.

- Deletes or clears buttons, or reports user button actions.

clickid.description: Button id or start of range (for delete actions).

clickmax.description: End of range for delete actions.

oneOf.description: Delete one button or a range of buttons.

Btc.description:
Sent when a user clicks a button.

- Reports the button id and click modifiers.

ucid.description: Connection that clicked the button (0 = local).

Btn.description:
Create or update a button.

- Buttons can include optional captions and input fields.
- Position and size are specified in screen-relative units.

caption.description: Optional caption displayed before the main text.

typein.description: Max chars permitted for a button with input.

ucid.description: Connection to display the button (0 = local, 255 = all).

oneOf.description: Text string, defaults to pale blue

Btt.description:
Sent when a user types into a text entry button.

- Includes the input text and the original `typein` limit.

ucid.description: Connection that typed into the button (0 = local).

CarContact.description: Contact details used by collision reports.

Cch.description: Camera Change - sent when an existing driver changes camera

reqi.description: Non-zero if the packet is a packet request or a reply to a request

Cim.description:
Connection interface mode report.

- Indicates which UI screen/mode a connection is in.

seltype.description:
Selected object type (0 if none).
Special values are used for InSim checkpoints, circles, marshal zones, and routes.

oneOf.description: Braking and traction control tab of setup screen
Drive / gear tab of setup screen

Cnl.description:
Connection left notification.

- Reports the reason and current connection count.

CnlReason.description: Used within [Cnl] to indicate the leave reason.

oneOf.description: Join out of sync - initial sync failed

node.description: Index of the last node the player passed.

Con.description:
Vehicle-to-vehicle contact report.

- Sent when collision reporting is enabled in [IsiFlags](crate::insim::IsiFlags).

ConcreteAngle.description:
Represents Angle from 5.625 to 90 degrees in 5.625 steps.
Naming simplifies the fractional decimals for readability.

ConcreteHeight.description:
Represents Height in 0.25m steps (0.25m to 4.0m)
Using specific enum variants allows IDE autocomplete to guide the user.

ConcretePitch.description: Represents Pitch from 0 to 90 degrees in 6-degree steps.

ConnectionId.description:
Unique Connection Identifier (UCID).

Assigned by LFS when a client connects and remains stable for the lifetime of that
connection. Use [`ConnectionId::LOCAL`] (`0`) to refer to the host/local connection
and [`ConnectionId::ALL`] (`255`) when targeting all connections.

Note: a connection is not the same as a player - a connection can spectate, join,
pit, and rejoin. Each time it joins it gets a new [`super::PlayerId`], but the
[`ConnectionId`] never changes.

ControlKind.description:
Control Kind
Kind of Control Object

Coordinate.description:
Position in LFS world space.

- Internal units use fixed-point where 65536 = 1 meter.
- Use the `*_metres()` helpers for human-scale values.
- Optional `glam` conversions are provided.

Cpp.description:
Camera Position Pack reports the current camera position and state. This packet may also be
sent to control the camera.

reqi.description: Non-zero if the packet is a packet request or a reply to a request

time.description: Time in ms to get there (0 means instant)

viewplid.description: Unique ID of viewed player (0 = none)

Cpr.description:
Connection rename or plate change notification.

- Sent when a player changes their name or number plate.

ucid.description: Connection that changed name or plate.

Crs.description:
Car reset event or reset request.

- Sent when a car is reset, or used to request a reset.

plid.description: Player that was reset (or should be reset).

Csc.description:
Car state changed event.

- Reports start/stop transitions for a car.

Fin.description:
Provisional finish notification.

- Not a final result; use [Res](super::Res) for confirmed results.

Flg.description:
Flag state change for a player.

- Reports when blue or yellow flags are applied or cleared.

Fuel200.description: Fuel percentage with the `/showfuel` multiplier applied.

GameVersion.description:
LFS game version parsed from the version string.

- Parses values like `0.7F` and `0.7E15`.
- `major` is the numeric prefix; `minor` is the letter; `patch` is optional digits.
- Display and encoding use the canonical string form.

GetZEntry.description:
An entry in an [`PmoAction::GetZ`] request or response.

Send entries with Zbyte set to 240 to get the highest point at X, Y,
or use the approximate altitude. In the reply, Zbyte is adjusted and
`adjusted` indicates whether the adjustment succeeded.

adjusted.description: True if Zbyte was successfully adjusted (reply only).

xyz.description: Position (X, Y, input or output Z).

Hcp.description:
Per-vehicle handicap settings.

- Applies mass and intake restrictions per car model.
- Use [`set`](Hcp::set) and [`get`](Hcp::get) for [Vehicle] indexing.

info.description: Handicaps for each car model (indexed by [Vehicle]).

HcpCarHandicap.description: Handicap settings for a single vehicle.

Heading.description:
Angular measurement stored as radians (f64).

- 0° points along world Y, 90° along world -X.
- Values are not auto-normalized; use `normalize()` when needed.
- Includes object-heading wire conversions.

Represents heading or direction in LFS game space. Internally stored as f64 radians
for consistency across the codebase, with convenient conversions to/from degrees.
Uses double precision to minimize cumulative errors in physics calculations and
integration over time.

# Orientation
- 0 = world Y direction** (forward/north)
- 90 = world -X direction** (left/west)
- 180 = -Y direction** (backward/south)
- 270 = world X direction** (right/east)

Angles increase in the anti-clockwise direction when viewed from above.

# Wraparound
Angles are **not** automatically normalized. A Heading created from 450 will
preserve that value internally. Use [`normalize()`](Heading::normalize) to wrap
angles to the 0->360 range if needed.

```
use insim_core::heading::Heading;

let over = Heading::from_degrees(450.0);
assert!((over.to_degrees() - 450.0).abs() < 0.0001);  // Preserved as-is

let normalized = over.normalize();
assert!((normalized.to_degrees() - 90.0).abs() < 0.0001);  // Wrapped to [0, 360)
```

# Examples
```
use insim_core::heading::Heading;

let forward = Heading::from_degrees(0.0);    // Y direction
let left = Heading::from_degrees(90.0);      // -X direction
let backward = Heading::from_degrees(180.0); // -Y direction

assert_eq!(forward.to_degrees(), 0.0);
assert_eq!(left.to_degrees(), 90.0);
```

Hlv.description:
Hot lap validity violation report.

- Sent when HLVC is enabled in [IsiFlags](crate::insim::IsiFlags).

Iii.description:
Message sent by a user with the `/i` command.

- Delivered to the host's InSim connection.
- Contains the raw message text.

index.description: Circle index (stored in heading byte on wire)

Ipb.description:
IP ban list (host only).

- Used to set or retrieve banned IP addresses.

Isi.description:
InSim init/handshake packet sent to start communication.

- Sends initial connection options (which updates you want and how often).
- Controls where updates are delivered when using UDP.
- Requests a version reply when `reqi` is non-zero.

flags.description:
Connection options (e.g., NLP/MCI updates, collision reports, join handling).

Prefer [Mci](super::Mci) over [Nlp](super::Nlp) as it includes all NLP data.

iname.description: Short program name shown to LFS.

prefix.description:
Special host message prefix (single ASCII character).
Messages typed with this prefix are forwarded to InSim and not shown in chat.

reqi.description: If non-zero, requests a [crate::insim::Ver] reply and is echoed back.

udpport.description:
UDP port for replies.
- UDP: `0` = reply to the source port; non-zero = reply to this port.
- TCP: `0` = NLP/MCI over TCP; non-zero = NLP/MCI over UDP.

version.description:
Protocol version your program expects.
Defaults to [crate::VERSION].

Ism.description:
Multiplayer host information.

- Sent when a host is started or joined.
- Can be requested via [`TinyType::Ism`](crate::insim::TinyType::Ism).

host.description: Whether we are the host (true) or a guest (false).

Jrr.description:
Join Request Reply
Set the ISF_REQ_JOIN flag in the IS_ISI to receive join requests

A join request is seen as an IS_NPL packet with ZERO in the NumP field
An immediate response (e.g. within 1 second) is required using an IS_JRR packet
In this case, PLID must be zero and JRRAction must be JRR_REJECT or JRR_SPAWN
If you allow the join and it is successful you will then get a normal IS_NPL with NumP set
You can also specify the start position of the car using the StartPos structure

IS_JRR can also be used to move an existing car to a different location
In this case, PLID must be set, JRRAction must be JRR_RESET or higher and StartPos must be set

reqi.description: Non-zero if the packet is a packet request or a reply to a request

oneOf.description: Move the player, but do not repair
Use the default spawn location selected by LFS.
Use a custom spawn location and heading.

Lap.description:
Lap timing information for a player.

- Sent when a lap is completed.

etime.description: Total elapsed time since session start.

LapTimingInfo.description: Lap timing configuration for the current session.

oneOf.description: Standard lap timing with a given number of checkpoints.
No lap timing (open configuration without checkpoints).

License.description:
LFS content license tier.

- Determines which tracks and vehicles are available.
- See <https://www.lfs.net/contents> for details.

Mal.description:
Restrict which mods can be used.

- Contains a list of allowed mod ids.

Mci.description:
Multi-car telemetry updates.

- Contains position, speed, and heading for a subset of players.
- Large grids are sent across multiple packets.

info.description: Telemetry entries for a subset of players.

Mod.description:
Screen mode configuration.

- Set resolution and refresh rate, or switch to windowed mode.

Msl.description:
Send a message to the local computer only.

- On a dedicated host this appears in the host console.
- On a client this appears only to the local player.

sound.description: Sound effect to play with the message.

Mso.description:
System and user messages reported by LFS.

- Variable-length packet with a message payload.
- `textstart` marks where user-entered text begins within `msg`.

msg.description: Full message text (may include name prefix before `textstart`).

plid.description: Player that sent the message (0 = use `ucid`).

textstart.description: Index of the first user-entered character within `msg`.

ucid.description: Connection that sent the message (0 = host).

MsoUserType.description: Source/type of a message reported by [Mso].

oneOf.description: Message received with the prefix character from [Isi](super::Isi).
Hidden message typed with the `/o` command.

Mst.description:
Send a message to LFS as if typed by a user.

- Supports chat messages and slash commands.

Msx.description:
Extended message (like [Mst](super::Mst), but longer).

- For chat text only (not for commands).

Mtc.description:
Send a message to a specific connection (host only).

- Can target a connection or a player.
- Supports a sound effect on delivery.

plid.description: Player to receive the message (0 = use `ucid`).

sound.description: Sound effect to play with the message.

ucid.description: Connection to receive the message (0 = host, 255 = all).

Nci.description:
Extra information about a connection (host only).

- Requires an admin password on the host.
- Can be requested via [`TinyType::Nci`](crate::insim::TinyType::Nci).

Ncn.description:
Connection joined notification.

- Sent when a connection joins the host.
- Can be requested via [`TinyType::Ncn`](crate::insim::TinyType::Ncn).

admin.description: Whether the connection has admin privileges.

total.description: Total number of connections including host.

ucid.description: Connection identifier for the new connection.

Nlp.description:
Node/lap updates without positional coordinates.

- Similar to [Mci](super::Mci) but without coordinates.
- May be requested via [`TinyType::Nlp`](crate::insim::TinyType::Nlp).

info.description: Node, lap, and position for each player.

Npl.description:
Player joined race notification.

- Sent when a player joins the race (or returns from pits).
- Can be requested via [`TinyType::Npl`](crate::insim::TinyType::Npl).

config.description:
Vehicle configuration selection.

- UF1 / LX4 / LX6: 0 = default, 1 = open roof.
- GTR racing cars: 0 = default, 1 = alternate.

nump.description: Total number of players in the race.

plid.description: Player identifier assigned for this race.

Obh.description:
Object hit report.

- Sent when object hit reporting is enabled in [IsiFlags](crate::insim::IsiFlags).

Oco.description:
Start light control packet.

- Controls main or layout start lights.

oneOf.description: Give up control of all lights
Use Data byte to set the bulbs
Give up control of the specified lights

Pen.description:
Penalty state change for a player.

- Reports a penalty being applied, updated, or cleared.

oneOf.description: Stop-go in pit stop too short

Pfl.description:
Player flags changed.

- Reports changes to assist and input settings.

Pit.description:
Pit stop at the garage (not a teleport).

- Reports work done and tyres used.

tyres.description: Tyre compounds used (rear-left, rear-right, front-left, front-right).

PitLaneFact.description: Pit lane event type used by [Pla].

plid.description: Player involved in the pit lane event.

PlayerHandicap.description: Handicap settings for a single player.

PlayerId.description: Unique Player Identifier, commonly referred to as PLID in Insim.txt

Plc.description:
Restrict which standard vehicles a connection may select.

- Applies to standard (non-mod) vehicles only.

PlcAllowedCarsSet.description: Set of allowed standard vehicles for [Plc].

Plh.description:
Player handicap updates.

- Sets or reports per-player handicaps.

PmoAction.description:
The action carried by an [`Axm`] packet.

Action-specific flags (e.g. [`PmoFlags::SELECTION_REAL`] for [`Selection`](PmoAction::Selection))
live on [`Axm::flags`] rather than inside the variant.

oneOf.description: Sent by the layout loading system while loading a file.
Remove all objects from the layout.
Set or report a connection's layout editor selection.

User pressed 'O' without anything selected; reports current editor position.

Information only - no object is added. Only `xyz` and `heading` are meaningful.

Request or reply with Z values for given X, Y positions.

Send entries with suggested Zbyte values; receive them back with adjusted
Zbyte values and [`GetZEntry::adjusted`] set to indicate success.

RaceLaps.description: Handles the rules around how RaceLaps are described within Insim automatically for you.

oneOf.description: This is a fixed number of laps

This is a time-based event.

Valid values are `1..=48`. Values outside that range are clamped when encoding.

Reo.description:
Starting order information or instruction.

- Sent at the start of a race or qualifying session.
- Can be requested via [`TinyType::Reo`](crate::insim::TinyType::Reo).

nump.description: Number of players in the order list.

RequestId.description:
Request Identifier (ReqI).

Controls reply correlation. When you send a packet with a non-zero `reqi`, LFS echoes
that value back in its reply so you can match the response to your original request.
When `reqi` is `0`, LFS may still send a reply but with `reqi = 0`, making it
indistinguishable from an unsolicited packet of the same type.

Use [`crate::WithRequestId`] to attach a request id without constructing the full
packet struct by hand:

```rust,ignore
use insim::{WithRequestId, insim::TinyType};
connection.send(TinyType::Ncn.with_request_id(1));
```

The echo value has no special meaning beyond correlation - any non-zero `u8` works.
Many applications simply always use `1` when they don't need to track multiple
in-flight requests simultaneously.

(Referred to as `ReqI` in InSim.txt)

Res.description:
Confirmed race or qualifying result.

- Includes player identity, car, and timing details.

resultnum.description: Finish or qualify position (0 = win, 255 = not in table).

Rip.description:
Replay control and status packet.

- Used to request replay actions and receive replay status.
- Can be requested via [`TinyType::Rip`](crate::insim::TinyType::Rip).

ctime.description: Requested destination time (request) or current position (reply).

rname.description: Replay name (empty when querying current replay).

ttime.description: Replay length (reply) or zero when requesting.

oneOf.description: Can't run a replay - dedicated host
Can't start a replay - not in a suitable mode
RName is zero but no replay is currently loaded
Replay search was terminated by user
Can't reach destination - SPR is out of sync

route.description: Route index (stored in heading byte on wire)

Rst.description:
Race start information and session configuration.

- Sent when a race or qualifying session begins.
- Can be requested via [`TinyType::Rst`](crate::insim::TinyType::Rst).

finish.description: Node index for the finish line.

numnodes.description: Total number of nodes in the path.

nump.description: Number of players in the race.

Scc.description:
Set the in-game camera for a player.

- Updates the camera view for the target player.

Sch.description:
Send a single key press.

- Best for simple keys; some special keys may not work.

Set.description:
Player sent setup to host

- Sent when SET is enabled in [IsiFlags](crate::insim::IsiFlags).

setup.description:
Raw setup bytes. Same format as SET file, with the following exceptions:
- less the first 12 bytes
- gear order is the first 7 gears then final drive ratio

Sfp.description:
Set a game state flag.

- Updates a single [StaFlags] value.
- Some states can only be toggled via text commands.

flag.description: State flag to set or clear.

onoff.description: Whether to enable (true) or disable (false) the flag.

Slc.description:
Connection selected a car.

- Sent when a connection selects a car.

Small.description:
General purpose packet carrying a subtype and an integer value.

- Used for requests, replies, and small control messages.
- The meaning depends on `subt`.

subt.description: Subtype describing the request or value.

oneOf.description: Request node and lap updates at the specified interval.
Request outgauge-style updates at the specified interval.
Set the node/lap interval used for [Nlp](super::Nlp).
Set or reply with allowed cars.
Set local car switches (signals, horn, siren).
Request local AI info for the given player id.

SoundType.description: Sound effect used when delivering [Msl] or [Mtc](super::Mtc) messages.

Speed.description:
Speed stored as meters per second.

- Internal units are m/s.
- Helper methods convert to and from kph and mph.

Spx.description:
Split timing for a player.

- Sent when a player crosses a split.

etime.description: Total elapsed time since session start.

Ssh.description:
Screenshot request and response.

- Send to request a screenshot, receive to learn the saved filename.

oneOf.description: This is a dedicated server. Screenshot unavailable.

Sta.description:
Game state snapshot.

- Sent when state changes.
- Can be requested via [`TinyType::Sst`](crate::insim::TinyType::Sst).

ingamecam.description: Selected in-game camera (even if free view is active).

numfinished.description: Number of finished or qualifying players.

replayspeed.description: Replay playback speed (1.0 is normal speed).

serverstatus.description: Server status indicator (1 = ok, 0 = unknown, >1 = failure).

Tiny.description:
General purpose request/reply packet.

- Used for lightweight requests and notifications.
- The meaning is defined by the `subt` value.

subt.description: Subtype describing the request or notification.

oneOf.description: Vote cancelled or request vote cancellation.

Toc.description:
Driver swap notification.

- Indicates a player moved between connections.

newucid.description: New connection id for this player.

Track.description:
Track identifier and metadata.

- `code()` returns the LFS track code (e.g., `BL1`, `SO4R`).
- `license()` reports the required content tier.
- `is_reverse()` / `is_open()` are derived from the track code.

Ttc.description:
Target-to-connection packet for selection-related requests.

- Routes a request to a specific connection.
- The meaning of `b1`..`b3` depends on the subtype.

ucid.description: Connection unique id to target (0 = local).

oneOf.description: Request the current layout editor selection ([`Axm`](crate::insim::Axm)).

Uco.description:
User control object event.

- Reports crossing a checkpoint or entering/leaving a circle.

Vector.description:
3D vector used for motion and forces.

- Units depend on context (velocity, acceleration, direction).
- Use the optional `glam` conversions when enabled.

Vehicle.description:
Vehicle identifier for standard cars and mods.

- Standard vehicles use 3-character codes (e.g., `XFG`).
- Mods are encoded as 6-character hex ids.
- `license()` reports the required content tier.

See <https://www.lfs.net/forum/thread/95662-New-InSim-packet-size-byte-and-mod-info>

Ver.description:
Version information about the connected LFS instance.

- Sent in response to [`TinyType::Ver`](crate::insim::TinyType::Ver).
- Also sent on connection if `Isi::reqi` is non-zero.

insimver.description: InSim protocol version reported by LFS.

reqi.description: Request identifier echoed from the request.

action.description: The action or fact for this vote notification

reqi.description: Non-zero if the packet is a packet request or a reply to a request

ucid.description: The unique connection id of the connection that voted

VtnAction.description: Enum for the action field of [Vtn].

Wind.description:
Wind strength levels.

- Discrete values (not a continuous scale).
- Reported in session state packets.

description:
Enum representing all possible packets receivable via an Insim connection.
Each variant may either be instructional (tell LFS to do something), informational (you are
told something about LFS), or both.
title: Packet
